domain: Tree-frog wet-state toe-pad — hexagonal-cell pads with mucus-filled channels; wet capillary adhesion grips strongly only when wetting condition is satisfied; same pad slides on dry rough surface.
specific_mechanism: Adhesion is conditional on global wet-state; per-pillar dynamics secondary to whole-pad wetting regime.
llm_application: CAPILLARY-GATE: context-window-level scalar 'moistness' signal toggles attention regime between free-running (low gain) and strong-capillary (high gain). One scalar per context window, not per token. Side-detector computes context relevance / specificity; the resulting scalar multiplies attention strength uniformly within the window.
novelty_claim: Gated Attention (2505.06708) gates per-head per-token via Q/K. GLA applies gating to value-stream. Context-aware retrieval uses separate retrieval models. CAPILLARY-GATE's distinctive contribution is a WINDOW-LEVEL SCALAR (not per-token, not per-head) that uniformly toggles attention regime — context-window-state gating rather than fine-grained token-level gating.
